entity_name: A.C.N. 615 641 079 Pty Ltd trading as Australian Home and Community Care; SIL Finder
description_text: On 11 January 2023, a banning order was issued under sections 73ZN(1)(b)(i) and 73ZN(1)(b)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, permanently prohibiting A.C.N. 615 641 079 Pty Ltd from providing NDIS supports and services to people with disability, effective from 5:00 pm on 19 January 2023. The banning order was issued on grounds provided under sections 73ZN(1)(b)(i) and 73ZN(1)(b)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, in that a delegate of the NDIS Commissioner has formed a reasonable belief that A.C.N. 615 641 079 Pty Ltd has breached the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 and is not suitable to be involved in providing supports or services to people with disability. Conduct identified includes non-compliance with the NDIS Code of Conduct through a failure to act with integrity, honesty and transparency. The NDIS Commission identified conduct involving the submission of falsified and inappropriate claims for payment to the NDIS for services not delivered and in collusion with other entities and individuals, which had serious adverse effect on NDIS participants’ mental and physical wellbeing.

description_text: On 2 August 2023, a suspension notice was issued to Allcare Disability Group Pty Ltd suspending the provider’s registration for a period of 30 days, effective from 14 August 2023. The suspension of registration was based on sections 73N(1)(a) and 73N(1)(d) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (NDIS Act). This includes the grounds that, a delegate of the NDIS Commissioner, formed a reasonable belief that Allcare Disability Group has contravened the NDIS Act and is no longer suitable to provide supports or services to people with disability. The identified contraventions of the NDIS Act were:• a failure to notify the Commissioner of a significant change in the organisation or governance arrangements of Allcare Disability Group Pty Ltd, in accordance with section 13A of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018; • a failure to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed on Allcare Disability Group Pty Ltd’s registration requiring engagement of an approved quality auditor;• a failure to commence a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018; and providing false and misleading information in application for registration.

description_text: On 16 December 2024, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(1)(b)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Assistive Disability Services Pty Ltd from engaging in the provision or management of NDIS supports, services or funding to people with disability within the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), including but not limited to: 1. Engaging in activities that an NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; 2. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken as a consultant, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services;3. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken as an auditor for an NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services; and4. Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role as a provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS.This order takes effect on 10 January 2025 at 5:00pm (AEDT) for a period of ten (10) years and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

description_text: On 22 December 2025 a notice of revocation of registration was issued to Auspicare Pty Ltd under section 73P(1)(a) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (NDIS Act). The revocation takes effect from 19 January 2026. Auspicare Pty Ltd can no longer operate as registered NDIS provider, including providing specialist behaviour support, specialist disability accommodation, plan management services, any class of support or service to a person whose NDIS plan is managed by the NDIA and implementing regulated restrictive practices. The revocation decision was made on the basis that a delegate of the Commissioner reasonably believes that Auspicare Pty Ltd has contravened, is contravening, or is proposing to contravene the NDIS Act. In forming this reasonable belief, the delegate had regard to outcomes of an audit undertaken of Auspicare Pty Ltd and which identified a number of major non-conformities with applicable NDIS Practice Standards and which Auspicare Pty Ltd is alleged to have failed to rectify.

On 19 January 2026 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(b) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Ayobami Okikiola Olawumi from engaging in the following activities: • Provide or be involved or assist in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any supports and/or services under a Participant’s Plan and which: a. involves contact with NDIS participants, whether in a NDIS participant's home or elsewhere; or b. involves you accessing or receiving a NDIS participant's personal or financial information. description_text: On 4 February 2025, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, permanently prohibiting Brittany Webster from using any of the restrictive practices as described and listed by the Victorian Senior Practitioners Office (VSPO) as being prohibited.These prohibited restrictive practices include the following as per the directions of the VSPO:(a) the use of prone restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-down position)(b) the use of supine restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-up position)(c) pin downs (subduing a person by holding down their limbs or any part of the body, such as their arms or legs)(d) basket holds (subduing a person by wrapping your arm/s around their upper and or lower body)(e) takedown techniques (subduing a person by forcing them to free-fall to the floor or by forcing them to fall to the floor with support)(f) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of restraining or inhibiting a person’s respiratory or digestive function(g) any physical restraint that has the effect of pushing the person’s head forward onto their chest(h) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of compelling a person’s compliance through the infliction of pain, hyperextension of joints, or by applying pressure to the chest or joints This order takes effect from 5pm AEDST on the 6 February 2025.

Engaging in activities that a NDIS provider would undertake, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken as a consultant, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services. Engaging in activities that would be undertaken as an auditor for a NDIS provider, to the extent that those activities relate to NDIS supports or services. Engaging in activities in a non-service and non-support delivery role as a provider, to the extent that those activities have a direct effect on the provision of NDIS funded supports or services to people with disability in the NDIS. Providing or be involved or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any support(s)or service(s) under a Participant’s Plan. Engaging in any work or providing or performing any support(s) or service(s) (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and a NDIS Participant. description_text: On 7 November 2025, a permanent banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Fatoumata Nawah from being involved in the provision of supports or services to people with disability. This order has been issued on reasonable grounds that Fatoumata Nawah has contravened section 6(1)(d) and 6(1)(f) of the NDIS Code of Conduct Rules 2018, thereby contravening section 73V of the NDIS Act 2013 and is not suitable to be involved in the provision of supports or services to people with disability, per section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) of the NDIS Act 2013. Specifically, the order prohibits Fatoumata Nawah from being engaged in the following NDIS activities: 1. Engage in any work or provide or perform any service or services (whether paid or unpaid), for or on behalf of any NDIS Provider, which may occasion any personal contact between you and an NDIS Participant.This permanent banning order takes effect on 12 November 2025 at 5:00pm AEST and operates across all States and Territories of Australia.

description_text: On 19 January 2026 a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(iii) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 prohibiting Felicity Brazher from providing, being involved in or assisting in the provision, to any NDIS Participant, of any supports and/or services under a Participant’s Plan and which: a. Involves contact with NDIS participants, whether in a NDIS participant's home or elsewhere; or b. Involves accessing or receiving a NDIS participant's personal or financial information. In this instance: • 'Contact' means face-to face contact, oral communication, written communication or electronic communication, where you are physically touching the participant, speaking with, or building a rapport with the participant and • NDIS Participant means a participant within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act and • NDIS Provider means a NDIS Provider within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act and • Participant’s Plan means a plan within the meaning of section 9 of the NDIS Act The banning order is for a period of five (5) years effective from 5:00pm 28 January 2026. The banning order operates across all states and territories of Australia. The decision to make a banning order had regard to matters including a NDIS Worker Screening exclusion issued to Felicity Brazher and relevant criminal history.

description_text: On 25 July 2023, a notice of revocation of registration was issued to Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd under sections 73P(1)(a) and 73P(1)(d) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013. The revocation takes effect from 5:00pm on 8 August 2023

On 1 June 2023, a suspension notice was issued to Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd suspending the provider’s registration for a period of 30 days, effective from 8 June 2023. The suspension of registration was based on sections 73N(1)(a) and 73N(1)(d) of the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013 (NDIS Act). This includes the grounds that, a delegate of the NDIS Commissioner, formed a reasonable belief that Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd has contravened the NDIS Act and is no longer suitable to provide supports or services to people with disability. The identified contraventions of the NDIS Act were: • a failure to notify the Commissioner of a significant change in the organisation or governance arrangements of Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd, in accordance with section 13A of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018; • a failure to comply with an additional condition of registration imposed on Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd’s registration requiring engagement of an approved quality auditor; and • a failure to commence a mid-term audit in accordance with section 13B of the National Disability Insurance Scheme (Provider Registration and Practice Standards) Rules 2018. On 7 July 2023, a further suspension notice was issued to Lifeskills Partner Pty Ltd, suspending the provider’s registration for a period of 30 days, effective from 8 July 2023.

description_text: On 6 February 2025, a banning order was made under section 73ZN(2)(a)(i) the National Disability Insurance Scheme Act 2013, permanently prohibiting Simon Duden from using any of the restrictive practices as described and listed by the Victorian Senior Practitioners Office (VSPO) as being prohibited.These prohibited restrictive practices include the following as per the directions of the VSPO:(a) the use of prone restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-down position)(b) the use of supine restraint (subduing a person by forcing them into a face-up position)(c) pin downs (subduing a person by holding down their limbs or any part of the body, such as their arms or legs)(d) basket holds (subduing a person by wrapping your arm/s around their upper and or lower body)(e) takedown techniques (subduing a person by forcing them to free-fall to the floor or by forcing them to fall to the floor with support)(f) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of restraining or inhibiting a person’s respiratory or digestive function(g) any physical restraint that has the effect of pushing the person’s head forward onto their chest(h) any physical restraint that has the purpose or effect of compelling a person’s compliance through the infliction of pain, hyperextension of joints, or by applying pressure to the chest or joints This order takes effect from 5pm AEDST on the 6 February 2025.
